 {"id":"bd-328m","title":"[IDEA-02] RateLimitDeclarations: Manifest + SDK + Host Enforcement","description":"# RateLimitDeclarations: Manifest + SDK + Host Enforcement

## Goal
Make rate limits first-class, declared, and mechanically enforced across SDK, host, and CLI so agents can preflight capacity and connectors behave predictably under throttling.

## Background / Why
The Clawdbot synthesis explicitly calls out rate limit declarations as a cross-cutting need. There is a test bead (`bd-1idx`) but no end-to-end feature work that surfaces declarations in manifests, SDK, host discovery, and CLI outputs.

## Scope
- Extend manifest/operation schema to declare limits (per operation + per pool).
- SDK types + helper to emit runtime `RateLimitStatus`.
- Host discovery exposes limits; CLI surfaces them.

## Deliverables
- Manifest schema update for `rate_limits` on operations and/or connector pools.
- SDK helpers for checking and reporting `RateLimitStatus`.
- Host preflight and discovery include rate limit info.
- CLI `fcp connector info/introspect` prints limits.

## Tests
- Unit tests for schema validation and mapping.
- Integration tests for host discovery responses.
- E2E log coverage with structured log entries for rate limiting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rate limits are visible before invocation.
- Rate limit violations produce deterministic errors + Retry-After hints.
- CLI and JSON outputs include limit metadata.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:28:47.889479096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T16:46:52.583597998Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-328m","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:29:46.047798769Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-32k7","title":"TEST-AUDIT: Coverage inventory + mock usage map","description":"# TEST-AUDIT: Coverage inventory + mock usage map

## Goal
Produce a concrete coverage and mock-usage inventory so we can target real-component tests first.

## Deliverables
- Coverage map by crate and module: fcp-core, fcp-protocol, fcp-crypto, fcp-store, fcp-mesh, fcp-sandbox, fcp-host, fcp-cli, fcp-sdk, connectors.
- Mock/fake usage map: enumerate tests that rely on mock_server, wiremock, fake clients, or test doubles.
- Gap list for untested critical paths (protocol parse, crypto verification, revocation freshness, audit/receipt logic, sandbox enforcement).

## Notes
- This task does NOT delete existing mock tests; it produces a replacement plan for no-mock suites.

## Acceptance Criteria
- One report file under docs/testing/ with per-crate coverage estimate and mock usage list.
- Each gap maps to a follow-up bead in this epic.

 {"id":"bd-32sh","title":"[IDEA-28.1] Implement usage metric schema + host aggregation","description":"## What
Implement usage metric schema and host aggregation pipeline.

## How
- Define `UsageMetric` and optional `CostEstimate` objects.
- SDK helper to emit metrics from connectors.
- Host aggregates per zone and stores rolling windows.

## Success Criteria
- Aggregated metrics accessible via API/CLI.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Aggregation windows are deterministic and bounded.
- Metrics emit confidence labels for cost estimates.

 {"id":"bd-39gj","title":"E2E-SCRIPTS: Scenario matrix + runner + detailed logging","description":"# E2E-SCRIPTS: Scenario matrix + runner + detailed logging

## Goal
Provide a comprehensive set of E2E scripts plus a runner that executes them with consistent, detailed JSONL logging.

## Scope
- Scenario matrix: happy path, denial path, revocation, taint approval, offline repair, batch invoke, quota/rate limits, sandbox egress denial.
- Script runner that collects logs and validates JSONL schema.
- Summary report with pass/fail and timing per scenario.

## Constraints
- Scripts must use real fcp CLI and harness commands.
- Logs must include correlation IDs and artifacts per step.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runner can execute all scenarios locally with a single command.
- All scripts emit schema-compliant logs.
- Summary report includes pass/fail, duration, and key artifacts.

 {"id":"bd-39j0","title":"[IDEA-29.1] Implement egress policy explain CLI","description":"## What
Implement `fcp net explain --url` and reusable evaluator API.

## How
- Parse URL; apply NetworkConstraints evaluation in dry-run mode.
- Output allow/deny + reason code + suggested allowlist changes.

## Success Criteria
- No network calls; purely local evaluation.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Evaluator performs no network I/O.
- Suggests minimal allowlist change when blocked.

 {"id":"bd-3bus","title":"[IDEA-12] Structured Log Test Harness (fcp-testkit)","description":"# Structured Log Test Harness (fcp-testkit)

## Goal
Provide a reusable test harness that validates structured logs against the JSONL schema, enabling deterministic logging tests across crates.

## Background / Why
Testing/logging requirements demand JSONL logs, but tests currently validate logs ad hoc. A shared harness reduces duplication and enforces consistency.

## Scope
- Add log capture + schema validation helper in `fcp-testkit` or `fcp-conformance`.
- Provide examples for integration tests and E2E scripts.

## Deliverables
- `LogCapture` helper + `assert_logs_valid(schema)`.
- Example usage in one integration test.

## Tests
- Unit tests for the harness itself.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Logging validation can be enabled with a single helper call.","notes":"Extended fcp-testkit LogCapture to support test harness use: added push_line (manual JSONL line) and push_value (serde_json::Value -> JSONL) helpers; added unit tests validating schema success/failure and line numbering. fmt/check/clippy clean after changes.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"OliveDesert","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:39:12.376361299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T21:29:34.875837066Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bus","depends_on_id":"bd-11g2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:39:49.617428147Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3c0g","title":"[IDEA-25.1] Implement auto-tuning + presets","description":"## What
Implement parameter auto-tuning logic and preset table.

## How
- Evaluate MTU and path type.
- Select symbol size + repair factors from preset table.
- Expose config overrides.

## Success Criteria
- Default presets are applied deterministically based on path.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Selected parameters never exceed MTU safety limits.
- Overrides are validated and bounded.

-{"id":"bd-3drg","title":"[IDEA-08] Retry Taxonomy + FCP Error Mapping","description":"# Retry Taxonomy + FCP Error Mapping

## Goal
Standardize retryability classification and mapping to FCP error taxonomy across connectors.

## Background / Why
Discord and Telegram each implement custom retry classifiers. Consolidating into SDK avoids divergence and improves consistency (Retry-After, backoff with jitter, terminal errors).

## Scope
- SDK-level `RetryDecision` types.
- Helper to map external errors into FCP error codes + retry hints.

## Deliverables
- `RetryDecision` enum (Immediate, Backoff, After(Duration), Terminal).
- `RetryPolicy` helper that translates `RetryDecision` into backoff and logs.
- Error mapping guidance in docs.

## Tests
- Unit tests for mapping correctness.
- Integration test with simulated HTTP errors (429/5xx/parse errors).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Connectors can classify errors without ad-hoc string matching.
- Retry behavior is consistent across connectors.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:35:45.362878825Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T00:01:58.717146739Z","close_reason":"Implemented retry taxonomy helpers, mappings, and tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3drg","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:36:21.618496408Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3giu","title":"[IDEA-25.3] Benchmark harness + CI smoke","description":"## Scenario
- Run bench suite with preset configurations.
- Output JSON metrics artifact.

## Logging
- JSON summary with p50/p95/p99 for encode/decode.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:47:03.801753421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:27.165087595Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3giu","depends_on_id":"bd-3c0g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:47:09.348062424Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3giu","depends_on_id":"bd-f4sw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:47:03.808810212Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3giu","depends_on_id":"bd-mapp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:27.165054343Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3m2a","title":"TEST-CONNECTORS-LIVE: Real-service integration suites (manual)","description":"# TEST-CONNECTORS-LIVE: Real-service integration suites (manual)

## Goal
Add live integration tests for external services without mocks/fakes, using dedicated sandbox accounts.

## Scope
- Anthropic, OpenAI, Telegram, Discord, Twitter connectors.
- Real API calls with rate limits and secret handling.

## Constraints
- Must run as manual-only or gated by secrets in CI.
- No production accounts, no recording or fake fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each connector has a live test plan and minimal smoke tests.
- Logs redact secrets and include correlation IDs and cost/latency metadata.

 {"id":"bd-f4sw","title":"[IDEA-25] RaptorQ Parameter Auto-Tuning + Benchmarks","description":"## Background
Symbol size and repair parameters strongly impact performance and MTU safety. Current defaults are static and may be suboptimal per platform/path.

## Goals
- Add auto-tuning for symbol size and repair symbol count.
- Provide benchmark suite to compare configurations.
- Emit recommended parameters for common MTUs (LAN/DERP).

## Non-goals
- Changing the canonical wire format; only tune parameters.

## Approach
- Add a bench harness in `fcp-raptorq` and `fcp-cli bench` integration.
- Record decode/encode time, memory, and overhead.
- Store recommended presets in config.

## Risks
- Overfitting to benchmarks; keep conservative defaults and allow override.

## Success Criteria
- Bench suite produces consistent metrics across runs.
- Recommended parameters reduce p99 latency without MTU regressions.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Bench suite outputs p50/p95/p99 and memory usage JSON.
- Preset table covers LAN and DERP MTU defaults.

 {"id":"bd-fgot","title":"[IDEA-01.1] SDK runtime kit: trait surface + config schema","description":"## Goal
Define the minimal trait boundary and config schema for the runtime kit so streaming/polling supervisors can be used by connectors with platform-specific state.

## Details
- Add `SupervisorConfig` (backoff, jitter, retry budget, cooldown, shutdown timeout, max_consecutive_failures).
- Define connector-facing trait hooks:
  - `StreamingSession` (resume token, sequence, heartbeat state)
  - `PollingCursor` (offset/seq + persistence hooks)
- Define explicit health transition rules (Healthy → Degraded → Unhealthy) and expose `HealthSnapshot` updates.

## Tests
- Unit tests for config defaults and validation (reject nonsensical values).
- Compile-only tests in `fcp-sdk` to ensure trait ergonomics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Traits are stable enough to be used by both Discord- and Telegram-like connectors.
- Config defaults align with study docs (1s base, 60s cap, jitter on).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TopazBay","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:27:11.074137273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:31:01.425661178Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:31:01.425577503Z","close_reason":"Blocker flywheel_connectors-1n78.20 now closed. Implementation complete: SupervisorConfig, StreamingSession trait, PollingCursor trait, HealthTracker, 10 unit tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fgot","depends_on_id":"bd-2zy9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:27:11.086449529Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-fgot","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:28:06.767324440Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-fgot","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Complete (2026-01-28)

Implemented SDK runtime kit trait surface + config schema in crates/fcp-sdk/src/runtime.rs:

**Deliverables:**
- SupervisorConfig: backoff, jitter, retry budget, cooldown, shutdown timeout, heartbeat config
- StreamingSession trait: resume token, sequence, heartbeat, persistence hooks  
- PollingCursor trait: offset tracking, advance_if_newer, persistence hooks
- HealthTracker: state machine with explicit transition rules + auto-evaluation
- In-memory test implementations: InMemoryStreamingSession, InMemoryPollingCursor
- Prelude exports added

**Tests:** 10 unit tests passing
**Verification:** cargo clippy/fmt/test all clean

Ready to close when blocker flywheel_connectors-1n78.20 is closed.","created_at":"2026-01-28T17:16:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-gz2y","title":"[IDEA-27] Connector State Fork Resolution Workflow","description":"## Background
Stateful connectors must be single-writer; forks are safety incidents. We need detection and a structured resolution workflow.

## Goals
- Detect forked ConnectorStateObject chains.
- Pause connector execution and emit audit evidence.
- Provide tooling to resolve/merge or choose a head.

## Non-goals
- Automatic conflict resolution without operator input.

## Approach
- Add fork detection in state manager (same prev, divergent heads).
- Emit `ConnectorStateFork` audit event.
- Add CLI `fcp connector state resolve` to select resolution strategy.

## Risks
- Incorrect resolution could lose state; require explicit approval.

## Success Criteria
- Forks are detected deterministically and block further writes.
- Resolution workflow is auditable and reversible.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Fork detection pauses connector writes immediately.
- Resolution emits audit event referencing chosen head.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:48:13.199560131Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T06:14:57.580055031Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T06:14:57.579977367Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks completed (bd-rato, bd-3frf, bd-18af)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-hkhy","title":"[IDEA-17] Schema Hash + Canonical CBOR Vector Generator","description":"## Background
Spec and docs require deterministic CBOR + schema hash vectors, but maintaining them manually is error-prone. We need a generator to keep vectors and schema hashes in sync with the authoritative definitions.

## Goals
- Generate schema hashes and canonical CBOR vectors from source schemas.
- Emit stable JSON/CBOR fixtures for conformance tests.
- Integrate with CI to detect drift.

## Non-goals
- Auto-modify spec text or accept non-canonical encodings.

## Approach
- Add a generator tool in `fcp-conformance` (CLI subcommand).
- Parse schema definitions (CDDL/JSON schema or Rust type registry).
- Compute schema hash via existing canonicalization routines.
- Write vector files with deterministic ordering.

## Risks
- Schema evolution could break determinism; add fixtures and strict ordering rules.

## Success Criteria
- Generator produces identical outputs across runs/platforms.
- CI can regenerate and diff vectors without noise.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Regenerated vectors are byte‑for‑byte stable across two consecutive runs.
- Schema hash changes when a schema field definition changes.
- CI diff check fails on any vector drift.
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:41:36.306662418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T22:38:28.456055867Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-hkhy","title":"[IDEA-17] Schema Hash + Canonical CBOR Vector Generator","description":"## Background
Spec and docs require deterministic CBOR + schema hash vectors, but maintaining them manually is error-prone. We need a generator to keep vectors and schema hashes in sync with the authoritative definitions.

## Goals
- Generate schema hashes and canonical CBOR vectors from source schemas.
- Emit stable JSON/CBOR fixtures for conformance tests.
- Integrate with CI to detect drift.

## Non-goals
- Auto-modify spec text or accept non-canonical encodings.

## Approach
- Add a generator tool in `fcp-conformance` (CLI subcommand).
- Parse schema definitions (CDDL/JSON schema or Rust type registry).
- Compute schema hash via existing canonicalization routines.
- Write vector files with deterministic ordering.

## Risks
- Schema evolution could break determinism; add fixtures and strict ordering rules.

## Success Criteria
- Generator produces identical outputs across runs/platforms.
- CI can regenerate and diff vectors without noise.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Regenerated vectors are byte‑for‑byte stable across two consecutive runs.
- Schema hash changes when a schema field definition changes.
- CI diff check fails on any vector drift.

 {"id":"bd-j6ps","title":"[IDEA-22] Device Posture Attestation Enforcement","description":"## Background
The README and RFCs describe optional hardware-backed device posture attestations for high-trust zones, but enforcement is not yet mechanical.

## Goals
- Introduce `DevicePostureAttestation` objects and verification rules.
- Allow zone policies to require hardware-backed keys (TPM/Secure Enclave).
- Fail closed when posture is missing or invalid.

## Non-goals
- Implementing hardware attestation collection itself for every OS in this iteration (focus on verification and policy).

## Approach
- Define canonical schema for posture attestation.
- Extend enrollment validation and zone policy evaluation to require posture.
- Add test fixtures for valid/invalid posture.

## Risks
- Platform variance in attestation formats; keep schema extensible and versioned.

## Success Criteria
- Zones can enforce posture requirements with stable reason codes.
- Tests cover accept/deny paths.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Zones can enforce posture requirement with stable deny reason.
- Enrollment without posture fails closed in required zones.

 {"id":"bd-j7w1","title":"[IDEA-11] Streaming Health Signal Unification","description":"# Streaming Health Signal Unification

## Goal
Standardize how streaming connectors report health (heartbeat ack, zombie detection, reconnection) so hosts can interpret status consistently.

## Background / Why
Different connectors currently surface health in ad-hoc ways. A unified model improves monitoring, alerting, and auto-restart behavior.

## Scope
- Define health state transitions for streaming connections.
- Provide helper methods for health updates and log emission.

## Deliverables
- `StreamingHealthState` mapping to `HealthSnapshot`.
- Standard log fields for heartbeat/ack counts.

## Tests
- Unit tests for state transitions.
- Integration tests with a simulated streaming loop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Health signals are deterministic and consistent across connectors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:38:14.343854819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:38:52.139984190Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j7w1","depends_on_id":"bd-2zy9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:38:52.139949556Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k8z7","title":"[IDEA-03.3] Event ordering: examples + integration tests","description":"## Goal
Provide reference examples and tests for ordering metadata and sequentialization.

## Details
- Add an example mapping for Telegram (per chat) and Discord (gateway sequence).
- Integration test to ensure ordering metadata is preserved through event emission.

## Tests
- Integration tests using fake event sources.
- Logging assertions include `stream_key` + `sequence`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Example code is ready for copy/paste into connector implementations.
- Ordering metadata survives through SDK → host pipeline.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:30:54.197677499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:30:54.205321712Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k8z7","depends_on_id":"bd-xyyt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:30:54.205287879Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
